full_text:
## **GeoMx/tCyCIF integration 09.02.2026**

## **Quality Control (QC)**

- No additional QC required, excessive quality control performed by
 > Iga

## **Batch/Sample Effect Correction**

Batch effect correction has been performed already.

Technical noise has already been removed.

- Optional sample correction approaches:

 - **PRIMUS covariate** (Kaiyang?)

 - **Linear mixed models** with covariates:

 - limma

 - Harmony

 - ComBat

Correction for sample-specific effect may remove also the variance
important for our exploration. This question is not the most important
for our initial purposes.

## **Normalization**

- Q3 normalization (recommended by GeoMx) for Differential expression
 > analysis.

- No major differences observed compared to other methods (DeSeq2,
 > VST) when doing GSEA analysis and Differential Expression
 > analysis.

## **Deconvolution**

- Deconvolution is reliable for **abundant cell types (T cells,
 > fibroblasts, tumor cells)**

- Deconvolution outputs different values for different methods, but
 > the results are correlated.

- Results for **tumor cells** can be trusted -\> initial analyses
 > could be performed on deconvoluted tumor signal, if we want to
 > focus on tumor signaling.

## **Differential Expression & Pathway Analysis**

- **Differential Gene Expression (DGE):\
 > **

 - LIANA + the differential gene expression integrated to the GeoMx

signaling.

## **Differential Expression & Pathway Analysis**

- **Differential Gene Expression (DGE):\
 > **

 - LIANA + the differential gene expression integrated to the GeoMx
 > pipeline

- **Pathway analysis:\
 > **

 - GSEA package from R (or GSEA MDigsb)

## **Ligand--Receptor Analysis**

- Gayani has implemented **three ligand--receptor packages** (Iga can
 > share):

 - One designed for **bulk RNA-seq\
 > **

 - One for **single-cell data\
 > **

 - One that can be applied to **deconvoluted data\
 > **

- Additional tools:

 - MultinicheR (ligand--receptor analysis)

## **Statistical Analysis (n = 8 vs 8)**

The comparison has to be balanced :check in GSEA, ssGSEA, DE analysis
whether sample sizes influence the result.

- Compare groups based on phenotypes, e.g.:

 - CCND1+

 - Ki67+

 - pRb+

- Randomization approach:

 - Randomize Ki67− samples and compare against Ki67+ samples

 - Include **patient** and **group** as covariates

- Survival-based grouping:

 - Group by survival outcome

 - Adjust for **R0 status\
 > **

## **Tumor-Focused Analyses**

- Prioritize tumor signal first (many regions are TSI).

- Explore correlations with TSI:

 - Gene expression analysis

 - Using stroma as bulk or deconvoluted data

 - Apply the same approach to ligand--receptor analysis

## **Single-Cell Integration**

- Perform ligand--receptor analysis on scRNA-seq data.

roma as bulk or deconvoluted data

 - Apply the same approach to ligand--receptor analysis

## **Single-Cell Integration**

- Perform ligand--receptor analysis on scRNA-seq data.

- Validate results at the **compartment level**:

 - Use NKI project scripts

## **Relevant Code & Resources**

- **Färkkilä lab:\
 > **

 - roi_selection_quantification/1_ROI_selection/05_Analysis_of_labelled_ROIs.ipynb

- **Spatial proximity & TME exploration (Pablo's code):\
 > **

 - Spacestat:

 - Network analysis describing components formed around cancer
 > cells

 - Functional markers and expression analysis

 - Path: /spacestat
